LAW AND MOTION 302,DEFENDANT GEORAPHIC EXPEDITONS, INC.'S MOTION TO DISMISS OR, IN THE ALTERNATIVE, TO STAY ACTION AND TO COMPEL ARBITRATION. THE MOTION TO COMPEL ARBITRATION IS DENIED. THE AGREEMENT TO ARBITRATE IS UNCONSCIONABLE UNDER ARMENDARIZ V. FOUNDATION HEALTH PSYCHARE SERVICES, INC. (2000) 24 CAL.4TH 83. UNDER THE FEDERAL ARBITRATION ACT, THE COURT HAS THE POWER TO DECLARE AN ARBITRATION CLAUSE UNCONSCIONABLE. SEE KLUSSMAN V. CROSS COUNTRY BANK (2005) 134 CAL.APP.4TH 1283, 1290-1291. THE AGREEMENT AS ISSUE IS BOTH PROCEDURALLY AND SUBSTANTIVELY UNCONSCIONABLE. THE SANO LETTER ESTABLISHES THAT THE AGREEMENT WAS PRESENTED AS A TAKE IT OR LEAVE IT PROPOSITION AND WAS ALSO REPRESENTED TO BE CONSISTENT WITH INDUSTRY PRACTICE. AS A CONSEQUENCE IF THE PLAINTIFF AND DECEDENT WISHED TO GO ON THIS TRIP, THEY COULD DO SO ONLY ON THESE TERMS. UNCONSCIONABILITY ALSO PERMEATES THE SUBSTANTIVE TERMS OF THE AGREEMENT TO ARBITRATE. THE PROBLEMATIC TERMS ARE THE LIMITATION ON DAMAGES, THE INDEMNITY OF GEOEX, THE REQUIREMENT THAT GEOEX COSTS AND ATTORNEYS' FEES BE PAID IF SUIT IS FILED RELATED TO CERTAIN CLAIMS, SPLITTING THE COSTS OF MEDIATION, THE ABSENCE OF AN AGREEMENT ON THE COST OF ARBITRATION AND THE LACK OF MUTUALITY AS TO EACH OF THESE TERMS. AS A CONSEQUENCE, THIS IS NOT A CASE WHERE THE COURT MAY STRIKE A SINGLE CLAUSE AND COMPEL ARBITRATION. ARMENDARIZ SUPRA 24 CAL.4TH AT 124. JUDGE: PATRICK J. MAHONEY, REPORTER: MARIA TORREANO, CSR 8600

RULING: Argued and the Court adopted its tentative ruling as discussed on the record.
Motion for further responses to Plaintiff's request for Production of Documents GRANTED as follows:
Request 10 Granted, Presentations, notes and logs regarding risk managment are relevant and reasonably calculated to lead to discoverable evidence.
Request 16, 18-20, 24, 28 and 43 (call logs) GRANTED IN PART. Request 37, 38, 41, 43 and 45 Motion Granted. The purpose of the litigation privilege is to bar derivative tort claim based on statements made during the course of litigation. Further, these reportS do not appear to have been made in anticipation of litigation but to review what occurred.
Request for sanctions is Denied.

APPEARANCES: David J. Bennion, Esq. of Law Offices of David J. Bennion 408/298-1948 for pltf & opposing party; Rodney E. Gould, Esq. of Rubin, Hay & Gould, P.C. 508/875-5222 for deft & moving party (appearing via CourtCall).
RULING: Argued and the Court adopted its tentative ruling - The motion to compel arbitration is denied. The agreement to arbitrate is unconscionable under Armendariz v. Foundation Health Psychare Services, Inc. (2000) 24 Cal.4th 83. Under the Federal Arbitration Act, the Court has the power to declare an arbitration clause unconscionable. See Klussman v. Cross Country Bank (2005) 134 Cal.App.4th 1283, 1290-1291. The agreement as issue is both procedurally and substantively unconscionable. The Sano letter establishes that the agreement was presented as a take it or leave it proposition and was also represented to be consistent with industry practice. As a consequence if the plaintiff and decedent wished to go on this trip, they could do so only on these terms. Unconscionability also permeates the substantive terms of the agreement to arbitrate. The problematic terms are the limitation on damages, the indemnity of GeoEx, the requirement that GeoEx costs and attorneys' fees be paid if suit is filed related to certain claims, splitting the costs of mediation, the absence of an agreement on the cost of arbitration and the lack of mutuality as to each of these terms. As a consequence, this is not a case where the Court may strike a single clause and compel arbitration. Armendariz supra 24 Cal.4th at 124. (302/PJM/PB) Judge: Patrick J. Mahoney, Reporter: Maria Torreano, CSR 8600
